title: BONCAT-FACS on River Water and Sewage Effluent Samples
author: Katharine Moss

steps.instruction: Prepare 100 mg/mL HPG solution by dissolving 100 mg of HPG in 1 mL of MilliQ water. Store solution at 4 °C in the dark.
To prepare 20 millimolar (mM) CuSO 4 . 5H 2 O solution, dissolve 0.5 g of CuSO 4 . 5H 2 O in 100 mL of MilliQ water. Store solution at 4 °C .

To prepare 1.15 Molarity (M) THPTA stock solution, dissolve 500 mg of THPTA in 1 mL of MilliQ water. To prepare 50 millimolar (mM) THPTA working solution, dilute 10 µL of stock solution in 220 µL of MilliQ water. Store solutions at -20 °C . Aliquot multiple working solutions to minimise the number of freeze/ thaw cycles of stock solution.

To prepare 6.5 millimolar (mM) Alexa Fluor 488 picolyl azide stock solution, dissolve 5 mg of Alexa Fluor 488 picolyl azide in 1 mL of molecular grade DMSO. To prepare 0.13 millimolar (mM) Alexa Fluor 488 picolyl azide working solution, dilute 10 µL of stock solution in 490 µL of molecular-grade DMSO. Store solutions at -20 °C in the dark. Aliquot multiple working solutions to minimise the number of freeze/ thaw cycles of stock solution.
Make fresh 100 millimolar (mM) sodium ascorbate solution. To prepare, dissolve 0.8 g of sodium ascorbate in 40 mL of 1X PBS.
Prepare 50 % (v/v) , 80 % (v/v) and 96 % (v/v) ethanol solutions with MilliQ water.
Make fresh 100 millimolar (mM) aminoguanidine hydrochloride solution. To prepare, dissolve 0.4 g of aminoguanidine hydrochloride in 40 mL of 1X PBS.

To 25 mL of river water, add 32 µL of 100 mg/mL HPG. Final concentration of HPG in sample is 1 millimolar (mM) . Incubate in the dark at Room temperature for 00:15:00 . It is recommended to include 3 to 4 replicates for each sample, and to include negative controls where no HPG is added. Negative controls are used to verify gating on a flow cytometer. To optimise HPG concentrations and incubation times, run samples in replicate over a range of final concentrations (e.g., 1 µM to 1 mM) and incubation times (e.g., 15 to 60 mins).
Fix sample with 25 mL of molecular-grade ethanol (final concentration 50 % (v/v) ). Incubate in the dark at Room temperature for 01:00:00 .
Before use, remove all reagents from fridge/ freezer and allow to come up to/ thaw at Room temperature .
Set up filter funnels (25 mm diameter) with PVDF filters (25 mm, 0.2 μm). Connect to vacuum pump or tap. Label funnels with sample names.
Condition filters with approximately 5 mL of 50 % (v/v) ethanol.
Filter the ethanol fixed samples onto the filters.
Release vacuum and cover filters with 0.5 mL of 80 % (v/v) ethanol. Incubate for 00:03:00 , then vacuum through the ethanol.
Release vacuum and cover filters with 0.5 mL of 96 % (v/v) ethanol. Incubate for 00:03:00 , then vacuum through the ethanol. Vacuum dry the filter for approximately 00:10:00 .

The volumetric amounts of reagents in steps 16 and 17 are for one sample's worth. It is recommended to make enough for n + 1 samples (n = number of samples). To make dye premix, add together 2.5 µL of CuSO 4 , 5 µL of THPTA and 19 µL of Alexa Fluor 488 picolyl azide. Invert tube. Incubate in the dark at Room temperature for 00:03:00 .

To dye premix, add 25 µL of sodium ascorbate, 25 µL of aminoguanidine hydrochloride, and 423.5 µL of 1X PBS. Invert tube. Final concentration of Alexa Fluor picolyl azide in reaction mixture is 5 micromolar (µM) . To maintain reducing conditions of the reaction mixture, do not vortex the mixture to mix the reagents. To optimise click dye concentrations and incubations times, run samples in replicate over a range of concentrations (e.g., 1 to 5 µM) and incubation times (e.g., 10 to 30 mins)
Release vacuum on filter funnels, and cover filters with 0.5 mL of reaction mixture. Incubate in the dark (e.g., cover funnels with foil) at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Vacuum through reaction mixture.
Release vacuum and cover filter with 0.5 mL of 1X PBS. Incubate for 00:03:00 , then vacuum through. Repeat twice more.
Repeat step 20 once with 50 % (v/v) ethanol.
Vacuum dry filters for approximately 00:10:00 .
With clean tweezers, place filters into 2 ml o-ring tubes with the top of the filter facing inwards. Wash tweezers with 70 % (v/v) ethanol between samples.
Add six clean stainless steel ball bearings to each tube. To clean stainless steel ball bearings, sonicate in 70 % (v/v) ethanol for 00:10:00 . Dry in oven at 40 °C .
Add 1.5 mL of 1X PBS to each tube.
Vortex/shake tubes at max speed for 00:03:00 .
Aliquot 1 mL of bacterial suspension into 2 ml o-ring tubes.
Add 1 mL of molecular-grade ethanol to suspension. Final ethanol concentration is 50 % (v/v) . Can be stored at -20 °C for up to 2 weeks.
Dilute 300 µL of ethanol-fixed sample in 200 µL of sterile-filtered 1X PBS

Run HPG-tagged samples according to step 30. Sort 500,000 events from the BONCAT positive gate in semi-purity mode. Collect sorted events in 5 ml flow cytometry containing tubes containing 1 mL of sterile-filtered 1X PBS. Maintain events per second below 3000 to ensure accurate sorting by lowering the sample pressure if need be. .

Run a negative control to determine the BONCAT positive gate on a SH800S cell sorter at sample pressure of 5. Draw the BONCAT positive gate to encompass less than 0.1% of the negative control population on a AF 488 vs. FSC density plot. Use a 488 nm laser for excitation, and set threshold to 2 on FSC.
Vacuum concentrate sorted populations to dryness using a vacuum concentrator.
Store pellets at -80 °C until DNA extraction.
For DNA extraction, follow the manufacturer's standard protocol for the Qiagen DNeasy UltraClean Microbial kit.
